summary: Verified the surgical regex fix in /app/frontend/scripts/generate-sitemap.js. Confirmed /app/frontend/public/sitemap.xml is well-formed XML (35 URLs), preview env serves it correctly to both browser & Googlebot UA, robots.txt is correct, generator handles missing-trailing-slash input AND backend-down fallback correctly, and backend pytest regression is still 107 passed + 2 skipped + 0 failed.

verification_results.xml_well_formed: PASS — python ET.parse() succeeded (would have hard-failed on previous /loc> corruption)
verification_results.first_url_correct: PASS — first <loc> is exactly 'https://cardost.in/' with proper </loc> closing tag
verification_results.all_35_locs_canonical: PASS — all 35 locs start with 'https://cardost.in/'; no host leaks; 35 <loc> opens / 35 </loc> closes (balanced)
verification_results.generator_run_against_preview_backend: PASS — exit 0, wrote 35 URLs, parses cleanly, mtime updated, first <loc>=https://cardost.in/
verification_results.regression_input_simulation: PASS — node test harness fed `<loc>https://preview.example</loc>` (no trailing slash) and `<loc>https://preview.example/</loc>` (with slash) and `<loc>https://preview.example/shop</loc>`. All three rewrote to `https://cardost.in/`, `https://cardost.in/`, `https://cardost.in/shop` with intact </loc> tags. No /loc> artefact. 3 opens / 3 closes balanced.
verification_results.preview_smoke_browser: PASS — GET https://stereo-connect-2.preview.emergentagent.com/sitemap.xml → HTTP 200, Content-Type=application/xml, valid XML, 35 URLs, first=https://cardost.in/
verification_results.preview_smoke_googlebot: PASS — same URL with Googlebot UA → HTTP 200, identical body (diff = no difference)
verification_results.robots_txt_regression: PASS — robots.txt contains 'Sitemap: https://cardost.in/sitemap.xml' and Disallow rules for /api/, /admin, /admin/, /checkout, /cart, /my-orders, /order/. No /api/seo/ reference.
verification_results.fallback_unreachable_backend: PASS — REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://does-not-exist-12345.invalid → exit 0, mtime UNCHANGED, existing public/sitemap.xml preserved, stderr logged 'keeping existing public/sitemap.xml'
verification_results.backend_pytest_regression: PASS — 107 passed, 2 skipped, 0 failed (baseline preserved, no backend code touched)

critical_code_review_comments: The callback-based regex in generate-sitemap.js (lines 79-83) is now safe. The capture group [^<]* correctly stops at '<', and the host-rewrite sub-regex /^https?:\/\/[^\/]+/ operates ONLY on the captured loc-text (which contains no angle brackets), so the earlier bleed into </loc> is structurally impossible.

Minor: pytest collection requires REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env var to be set at shell level (test_cardost_universal.py uses os.environ[...] without a default — by design per env-var rules, so this is correct behavior, just noting it for future runners).
robots.txt regression check confirms Sitemap directive still points to https://cardost.in/sitemap.xml — consistent with the fix scope.
test_credentials: Not needed for this regex fix verification — admin login not exercised.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sitemap regex fix is verified. The previous bug (greedy /[^\/]+/ that consumed '<' of </loc>) is structurally fixed by switching to a callback-based replacer that operates only on the [^<]* captured content. Preview env serves a valid 35-URL sitemap. If you need to re-verify after a future regen, run: python3 -c 'import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; ET.parse("/app/frontend/public/sitemap.xml"); print("OK")' and curl the preview URL. Production at https://cardost.in still has the broken file until user redeploys — by design, not in scope here.
rca of the issue: RCA: the original regex `xml.replace(/<loc>https?:\/\/[^\/]+/g, '<loc>'+SITE)` had a character class [^/]+ that matched any char except '/'. When source XML was `<loc>https://host</loc>` (no trailing slash on bare host), the regex greedily consumed `https://host<` (the '<' of </loc> is not '/'), replacing it with `https://cardost.in` and leaving the malformed remainder `/loc>`. Mitigation: replaced with a callback regex /<loc>([^<]*)<\/loc>/g that captures the entire loc content (the [^<] class stops at '<', so it cannot consume the closing tag), then rewrites the host portion of the captured text and re-emits a well-formed <loc>...</loc> tag. Also normalizes bare-host URLs to include trailing slash. Verified via Python ET.parse() and via node test harness against the exact regression input — both pass.
